query_normalized: llmxive follow-up: extending "rethinking cross-layer information routing in diffusion transformers" computer science
rationale: This paper satisfies criterion (a) as it empirically measures the same underlying mechanism—information flow routing within neural network layers/edges—and applies it to the domain of artificial neural networks, providing foundational evidence for how such flows can be analyzed and intervened upon, which is directly relevant to the user's work on cross-layer information routing in Diffusion Transformers.
